---
title: "CBI Presentation"
author: "Olivier Amacker"
date: 2026-07-24
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, ../assets/style/presentation.scss]
    center: true
    transition: fade
    incremental: false
    slide-number: true
    preview-links: auto
    navigation-mode: grid
    # auto-play-media: true
    footer: '<a href="../index.qmd" style="color: inherit; text-decoration: none;">Home</a> | <a href="../sources.qmd" style="color: inherit; text-decoration: none;">Sources</a> | CBI Presentation © 2026'
bibliography: ../../bachelor-thesis-report/bibliography.bib
---

# What Is The Goal Of This Presentation?

- Explain what I have done so far
- Showcase my main results

# What Have I Done So Far?

 - Reproduced the @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 paper
 - Perturbed fMRIPrep runs and assessed the {{< acr FC >}} matrices changes
 - Perturbed {{< acr FC >}} matrices extraction and assessed the {{< acr PCA >}} features extraction changes

# Reproduction Of The @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper (1/X)

The code used for this section is available online: [https://olivier.amacker.dev/bachelor-thesis/site/notebooks/npvr-simulation/](https://olivier.amacker.dev/bachelor-thesis/site/notebooks/npvr-simulation/){target="_blank"}

![NPVR notebook QR code](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/npvr-simulation/qr-code.svg){#fig-npvr-qr}

# Reproduction Of The @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper (2/X)

The goal of this paper is to quantify the {{< acr NPVR >}} across multiple fMRIPrep runs made with MCA floating point arithmetic perturbations.

Here is the formula for the NPVR:

$$NPVR = \frac{\sigma_{num}}{\sigma_{pop}}$$

Where $\sigma_{num}$ is the numerical variability and $\sigma_{pop}$ is the population variability.

# Reproduction Of The @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper (3/X)

::: {layout="[ [1, 1, 1], [1, 1, 1] ]"}

![Population distribution (mine)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/npvr-simulation/populations_plot.png){#fig-npvr-populations width="100%" height="150px"}

![Relation between the NPVR and the Cohen's d (mine)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/npvr-simulation/npvr_sample_size_cohens_delta_variation.png){#fig-npvr-sample-size width="100%" height="150px"}

![Relation between pop and num var (mine)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/npvr-simulation/npvr_dpop_dnum_variation.png){#fig-dnum-variation width="100%" height="150px"}

![Population distribution (paper)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/npvr-simulation-original/population.png){#fig-original-population width="100%" height="150px"}

![Relation between the NPVR and the Cohen's d (paper)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/npvr-simulation-original/cohens.png){#fig-original-cohens width="100%" height="150px"}

![Relation between pop and num var (paper)](../assets/presentations/cbi-presentation/npvr-simulation-original/NPVR.png){#fig-original-npvr width="100%" height="150px"}

:::

# Reproduction Of The @Alizadeh2025.12.22.695524 Paper (4/X)

Regarding the Relation between the NPVR and the Cohen's d differences, a Pull Request was open and merged into the paper's repository (see [here](https://github.com/mina94az/Numerical-Variability-of-functional-MRI-Graph-Measures/pull/3){target="_blank"})

# References

::: {#refs}
:::

# Annex
Here is how the NPVR is calculated in details:

$$
  \sigma_{num}^2 = \frac{1}{m} \sum_{j=1}^m \left[ \frac{1}{n-1} \sum_{i=1}^n \left( x_{i,j} - \bar x_{.,j}\right)^2\right]
$$

$$
  \sigma_{pop}^2 = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n \left[ \frac{1}{m-1} \sum_{j=1}^m \left( x_{i,j} - \bar x_{i,.}\right)^2\right]
$$

$$
  NPVR = \frac{\sigma_{num}}{\sigma_{pop}}
$$
